name: Active Scanning
description: Adversaries may execute active reconnaissance scans to gather information that can be used during targeting. Active scans are those where the adversary probes victim infrastructure via network traffic, as opposed to other forms of reconnaissance that do not involve direct interaction.
_text: Active Scanning Adversaries may execute active reconnaissance scans to gather information that can be used during targeting. Active scans are those where the adversary probes victim infrastructure via network traffic, as opposed to other forms of reconnaissance that do not involve direct interaction.

name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
description: Adversaries may attempt to exploit a weakness in an Internet-facing host or system to initially access a network. The weakness in the system can be a software bug, a temporary glitch, or a misconfiguration. Exploitation of CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) in Apache Log4j is a prime example.
_text: Exploit Public-Facing Application Adversaries may attempt to exploit a weakness in an Internet-facing host or system to initially access a network. The weakness in the system can be a software bug, a temporary glitch, or a misconfiguration. Exploitation of CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) in Apache Log4j is a prime example.

name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries. These interfaces and languages provide ways of interacting with computer systems and are a common feature across many different platforms. Most systems come with some built-in command-line interface and scripting capabilities.
mitigations: Disable or Remove Feature or Program
_text: Command and Scripting Interpreter Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries. These interfaces and languages provide ways of interacting with computer systems and are a common feature across many different platforms. Most systems come with some built-in command-line interface and scripting capabilities.

name: OS Credential Dumping
description: Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login and credential material, normally in the form of a hash or a clear text password. Credentials can be obtained from OS caches, memory, or files. Dumping LSASS memory is a common Windows technique.
_text: OS Credential Dumping Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login and credential material, normally in the form of a hash or a clear text password. Credentials can be obtained from OS caches, memory, or files. Dumping LSASS memory is a common Windows technique.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion. Compromised credentials may be used to bypass access controls placed on various resources on systems within the network.
_text: Valid Accounts Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion. Compromised credentials may be used to bypass access controls placed on various resources on systems within the network.

name: Phishing
description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems. All forms of phishing are electronically delivered social engineering. Phishing can be targeted (spearphishing) or sent at scale.
_text: Phishing Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems. All forms of phishing are electronically delivered social engineering. Phishing can be targeted (spearphishing) or sent at scale.

name: Application Layer Protocol
description: Adversaries may communicate using OSI application layer protocols to avoid detection/network filtering by blending in with existing traffic. Commands to the remote system may also be included in the protocol traffic between the client and server.
_text: Application Layer Protocol Adversaries may communicate using OSI application layer protocols to avoid detection/network filtering by blending in with existing traffic. Commands to the remote system may also be included in the protocol traffic between the client and server.

name: Data Encrypted for Impact
description: Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems or on large numbers of systems in a network to interrupt availability to system and network resources. They can attempt to render stored data inaccessible by encrypting files or data on local and remote drives and withholding access to a decryption key.
_text: Data Encrypted for Impact Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems or on large numbers of systems in a network to interrupt availability to system and network resources. They can attempt to render stored data inaccessible by encrypting files or data on local and remote drives and withholding access to a decryption key.

name: Remote Services
description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a service specifically designed to accept remote connections, such as telnet, SSH, and VNC. The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.
mitigations: Disable or Remove Feature or Program
_text: Remote Services Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a service specifically designed to accept remote connections, such as telnet, SSH, and VNC. The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.

name: Process Discovery
description: Adversaries may attempt to get information about running processes on a system. Information obtained could be used to gain an understanding of common software/applications running on systems within the network.
_text: Process Discovery Adversaries may attempt to get information about running processes on a system. Information obtained could be used to gain an understanding of common software/applications running on systems within the network.

name: Create Account
description: Adversaries may create an account to maintain access to victim systems. With a sufficient level of access, creating such accounts may be used to establish secondary credentialed access that do not require persistent remote access tools to be deployed on the system.
_text: Create Account Adversaries may create an account to maintain access to victim systems. With a sufficient level of access, creating such accounts may be used to establish secondary credentialed access that do not require persistent remote access tools to be deployed on the system.

name: Service Stop
description: Adversaries may stop or disable services on a system to render those services unavailable to legitimate users. Stopping critical services can inhibit or stop response to an incident or aid in the adversary's overall objectives to cause damage to the environment.
_text: Service Stop Adversaries may stop or disable services on a system to render those services unavailable to legitimate users. Stopping critical services can inhibit or stop response to an incident or aid in the adversary's overall objectives to cause damage to the environment.

name: Log4Shell
description: Apache Log4j2 2.0-beta9 through 2.15.0 (excluding security releases) JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages or log message parameters can execute arbitrary code loaded from LDAP servers when message lookup substitution is enabled.
india_context: Exploited in CBSE web servers running Apache in 2022-2024. Patch uptake in government sector was slow.
_text: Log4Shell Apache Log4j2 2.0-beta9 through 2.15.0 (excluding security releases) JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages or log message parameters can execute arbitrary code loaded from LDAP servers when message lookup substitution is enabled. Exploited in CBSE web servers running Apache in 2022-2024. Patch uptake in government sector was slow.

name: Log4Shell Bypass
description: It was found that the fix to address CVE-2021-44228 in Apache Log4j 2.15.0 was incomplete. Attackers could craft malicious input data using a JNDI Lookup pattern resulting in a denial of service (DOS) attack or remote code execution.
india_context: Government entities that patched Log4Shell quickly were still vulnerable due to incomplete patch.
_text: Log4Shell Bypass It was found that the fix to address CVE-2021-44228 in Apache Log4j 2.15.0 was incomplete. Attackers could craft malicious input data using a JNDI Lookup pattern resulting in a denial of service (DOS) attack or remote code execution. Government entities that patched Log4Shell quickly were still vulnerable due to incomplete patch.

name: Spring4Shell
description: A Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux application running on JDK 9+ may be vulnerable to remote code execution (RCE) via data binding. The specific exploit requires the application to run on Tomcat as a WAR deployment.
india_context: Several Indian e-governance portals use Spring-based Java applications.
_text: Spring4Shell A Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux application running on JDK 9+ may be vulnerable to remote code execution (RCE) via data binding. The specific exploit requires the application to run on Tomcat as a WAR deployment. Several Indian e-governance portals use Spring-based Java applications.

name: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset
description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service (server resource consumption) because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly, as exploited in the wild in August through October 2023.
india_context: CERT-In issued advisory CIAD-2023-0147 on this. Several IRCTC-facing load balancers impacted.
_text: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service (server resource consumption) because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly, as exploited in the wild in August through October 2023. CERT-In issued advisory CIAD-2023-0147 on this. Several IRCTC-facing load balancers impacted.

name: EternalBlue / MS17-010
description: The SMBv1 server in Microsoft Windows allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted packets. Exploited by WannaCry and NotPetya ransomware globally. Still active in air-gapped OT/SCADA networks.
india_context: 70%+ Indian government infrastructure runs end-of-life Windows. AIIMS Delhi 2022 ransomware used this vector. Legacy SCADA systems in power grids still unpatched.
_text: EternalBlue / MS17-010 The SMBv1 server in Microsoft Windows allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted packets. Exploited by WannaCry and NotPetya ransomware globally. Still active in air-gapped OT/SCADA networks. 70%+ Indian government infrastructure runs end-of-life Windows. AIIMS Delhi 2022 ransomware used this vector. Legacy SCADA systems in power grids still unpatched.

name: PAN-OS Command Injection
description: A command injection vulnerability in the GlobalProtect feature of Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS software for specific PAN-OS versions and distinct feature configurations may enable an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code with root privileges on the firewall.
india_context: NCIIPC issued advisory. Several Indian government perimeter firewalls use Palo Alto. Exploited in Operation MidnightEclipse.
_text: PAN-OS Command Injection A command injection vulnerability in the GlobalProtect feature of Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS software for specific PAN-OS versions and distinct feature configurations may enable an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code with root privileges on the firewall. NCIIPC issued advisory. Several Indian government perimeter firewalls use Palo Alto. Exploited in Operation MidnightEclipse.

title: SideWinder APT Group Targeting Indian Military and Government
description: CERT-In has observed targeted attacks by the SideWinder APT group (also known as Rattlesnake, T-APT-04) against Indian military establishments, government agencies, and critical infrastructure. The attack chain begins with spearphishing emails containing malicious RTF documents exploiting CVE-2017-11882. Post-exploitation uses a PowerShell-based RAT for persistence.
_text: SideWinder APT Group Targeting Indian Military and Government CERT-In has observed targeted attacks by the SideWinder APT group (also known as Rattlesnake, T-APT-04) against Indian military establishments, government agencies, and critical infrastructure. The attack chain begins with spearphishing emails containing malicious RTF documents exploiting CVE-2017-11882. Post-exploitation uses a PowerShell-based RAT for persistence. T1566 T1203 T1059 T1071 T1003

title: APT41 Targeting Indian Healthcare and Education Sector
description: CERT-In has identified a campaign by APT41 (Winnti, Double Dragon) targeting Indian healthcare institutions including AIIMS and hospital chains. The attack begins with active reconnaissance of patient portals, followed by exploitation of web application vulnerabilities (Log4Shell, Spring4Shell). The goal appears to be patient data exfiltration and ransomware deployment as a secondary objective.
_text: APT41 Targeting Indian Healthcare and Education Sector CERT-In has identified a campaign by APT41 (Winnti, Double Dragon) targeting Indian healthcare institutions including AIIMS and hospital chains. The attack begins with active reconnaissance of patient portals, followed by exploitation of web application vulnerabilities (Log4Shell, Spring4Shell). The goal appears to be patient data exfiltration and ransomware deployment as a secondary objective. T1595 T1190 T1059 T1003 T1486

title: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack Against Indian Web Infrastructure
description: Following global disclosure of CVE-2023-44487, CERT-In has observed exploitation attempts against Indian government web portals including IRCTC, NIC-hosted portals, and state government sites. Attackers are using Rapid Reset to cause denial of service. Patch immediately to Nginx 1.25.3+ or Apache 2.4.58+.
_text: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack Against Indian Web Infrastructure Following global disclosure of CVE-2023-44487, CERT-In has observed exploitation attempts against Indian government web portals including IRCTC, NIC-hosted portals, and state government sites. Attackers are using Rapid Reset to cause denial of service. Patch immediately to Nginx 1.25.3+ or Apache 2.4.58+. T1595 T1499

title: Ransomware Targeting Indian Power Sector OT Networks
description: CERT-In alerts power sector entities of a coordinated ransomware campaign targeting IT/OT convergence zones. Attackers use valid SCADA credentials (obtained via phishing or brute force) to access engineering workstations, then deploy ransomware affecting both IT systems and OT HMI interfaces. Power distribution SCADA from ABB and Siemens are primary targets. Recommend immediate network segmentation of OT from IT.
_text: Ransomware Targeting Indian Power Sector OT Networks CERT-In alerts power sector entities of a coordinated ransomware campaign targeting IT/OT convergence zones. Attackers use valid SCADA credentials (obtained via phishing or brute force) to access engineering workstations, then deploy ransomware affecting both IT systems and OT HMI interfaces. Power distribution SCADA from ABB and Siemens are primary targets. Recommend immediate network segmentation of OT from IT. T1566 T1078 T1021 T1486 T1489

title: CBSE Portal Compromise via Valid Admin Credentials
description: CERT-In has evidence of compromise of the CBSE examination portal using stolen admin credentials. The attacker performed reconnaissance (T1595) targeting the exam portal in January, exploited the Log4Shell vulnerability for initial access (T1190), used command execution to establish persistence (T1059), and was observed performing credential dumping (T1003) in February. The attack coincided with the CBSE board exam season (February-March). Immediate password reset and MFA enforcement recommended.
_text: CBSE Portal Compromise via Valid Admin Credentials CERT-In has evidence of compromise of the CBSE examination portal using stolen admin credentials. The attacker performed reconnaissance (T1595) targeting the exam portal in January, exploited the Log4Shell vulnerability for initial access (T1190), used command execution to establish persistence (T1059), and was observed performing credential dumping (T1003) in February. The attack coincided with the CBSE board exam season (February-March). Immediate password reset and MFA enforcement recommended. T1595 T1190 T1059 T1003

title: Supply Chain Attack on Indian Railway Ticketing Systems
description: CERT-In has identified a supply chain compromise affecting a third-party vendor for IRCTC ticketing integration. Malicious code was injected into the vendor's update mechanism, resulting in installation of a backdoor on railway ticketing servers. The backdoor communicates over HTTP/S to C2 infrastructure.
_text: Supply Chain Attack on Indian Railway Ticketing Systems CERT-In has identified a supply chain compromise affecting a third-party vendor for IRCTC ticketing integration. Malicious code was injected into the vendor's update mechanism, resulting in installation of a backdoor on railway ticketing servers. The backdoor communicates over HTTP/S to C2 infrastructure. T1195 T1071 T1136 T1057

title: Volt Typhoon Activity Observed in Indian Infrastructure
description: CERT-In has identified Volt Typhoon (Bronze Silhouette) TTPs in probing activity against Indian critical infrastructure including power grid and telecom. Unlike destructive campaigns, this appears to be pre-positioning for future disruption. Living-off-the-land techniques observed: use of built-in tools (certutil, netsh), no custom malware.
iocs: Compromised SOHO routers used as proxies
_text: Volt Typhoon Activity Observed in Indian Infrastructure CERT-In has identified Volt Typhoon (Bronze Silhouette) TTPs in probing activity against Indian critical infrastructure including power grid and telecom. Unlike destructive campaigns, this appears to be pre-positioning for future disruption. Living-off-the-land techniques observed: use of built-in tools (certutil, netsh), no custom malware. T1595 T1078 T1021 T1057 T1136
